page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 1843 GB of local disk storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the D48ds_v5 in the average to strong performance tiers. It demonstrates strong capabilities in Passmark database operations, scoring 21703.24, and Passmark CPU encryption tests, scoring 43321.1. Memory performance is also strong, with a Passmark memory score of 2757.12, supported by a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 2502.0 GB/sec. For general computational tasks, the instance achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 15064.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 48717.7.

This server is suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation, as evidenced by its clang compilation score of 36188.0 and static web serving throughput of 38.67 GB/sec. While it handles small-scale language model tasks efficiently, achieving 275.95 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff for larger models. Large-scale LLM text generation drops to 2.74 tokens/sec on a 70B model, indicating it is not optimal for heavy AI inference workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, running on a Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor offering 48 dedicated vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage. This instance delivers strong performance in database operations, memory-intensive tasks, and cryptographic operations, as reflected in its Passmark database score of 21703.24 and encryption score of 43321.1. It also handles small-scale language model inference efficiently. However, a notable tradeoff is its weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation, where it achieves only 2.74 tokens/sec on a 70B model. The D48ds_v5 is best suited for relational databases, enterprise applications, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage, optimized for database operations and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v5 features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local storage for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB RAM.
Includes 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong performance in database operations and cryptographic tasks.
Efficient handling of small-scale LLM inference and software compilation.
Performance tradeoff in large-scale LLM text generation due to lack of GPU acceleration.
Suited for relational databases, web serving, and enterprise applications.
